scan_reason: bioRxiv API and website blocked by egress proxy
previously_processed_papers_re_encounter: All papers from previous scans re-encountered; no new papers within 3-day window

new_papers_outside_window.title: Pathogenesis of H5N1 Clade 2.3.4.4b in dry Jersey cows following intramammary inoculation shows within-host compartmentalization
new_papers_outside_window.score_rationale: H5N1 pathogenesis study in dry cows showing tissue compartmentalization. Adds to the dairy H5N1 cluster but focuses on cellular/pathological level changes rather than specific molecular protein-protein interactions. Keywords matched: H5N1, pathogenesis.
new_papers_outside_window.annotation: H5N1 clade 2.3.4.4b causes severe disease in dry cows with within-host viral compartmentalization; tissue tropism study complements existing dairy H5N1 cluster

new_papers_outside_window.title: antigen-prime: Simulating coupled genetic and antigenic evolution of influenza virus
new_papers_outside_window.score_rationale: Computational tool for simulating antigenic evolution. Not focused on host-pathogen molecular interactions — purely bioinformatics/epidemiology tool. Low HPMI relevance.
new_papers_outside_window.annotation: Computational platform for modeling influenza antigenic drift; no molecular interaction content

version_updates_detected.title: Haemagglutinin 162-164 deletions enhance influenza B/Victoria virus fitness and virulence in vivo
version_updates_detected.note: v2 revision detected; previously scored in 2026-03-11 scan. IBV focus — not reviewed at Tier 2 in prior scan because full text was inaccessible and paper focuses on influenza B rather than A.

Confirms continued publication lull for influenza HPMI preprints observed since March 8.
